product.project_name: Battery Cell Anomaly Detection - Foundation Model
product.project_description: A computer vision project utilizing frozen DINOv3 backbones and parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) for detection and classification and perform ablation study
product.problem_statement: Identifying anomalous battery cells from images under severe class imbalance (normal vs abnormal) to ensure quality control, while maximizing f1-score or map metric. it also provide a basis for scale the project to other tasks and other datasets that might not facing imblance problem. also it compare the hyperparameter effects
product.target_users: Machine learning researchers and engineers studying or building parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) pipelines for using foundation models and ViT based models like DINO-vit to train models for detection and multi-label classification
key_goals: Implement parameter-efficient training using foundation models like DINOv3 for multi-label classification and object detection.
Analyze the impact of different hyperparameter choices (such as ranks, bottleneck dimensions, and loss functions) on performance.
Establish an ablation study framework to compare PEFT methods (LoRA, Adapters, VPT) vs frozen baselines.
Design a modular codebase structure that scales to other vision tasks, models, and datasets.
Maximize F1-score on classification and mAP on detection tasks for the anomalous class under high class imbalance.

design_principles: Modular and extensible architecture (for scaling to other tasks and datasets)
Performance and resource efficiency (via PEFT and frozen backbones)
Comprehensive metric reporting (f1-score, mAP, confusion matrices)
adding unit test and validation codes for each module is necessary

workflow.development_workflow: Spec-driven development with checklist checkpoints, accompanied by a permanent and growing unit test suite.
workflow.git_strategy: Feature branches (one branch per track/feature), with verification steps run on the branch, then merged into the main branch once complete.
workflow.verification_steps: Run pytest tests/ for unit testing and launch smoke training configurations (e.g., configs/test_smoke.yaml) to verify end-to-end training runs. Critical Note: On Farhad's local PC, use 'conda activate pytorch' and do not run heavy training. On the server, use conda environment '/home/jovyan/pytorch_env' where heavy training/ablations are allowed.
